name: Sourdough Bagels
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons Sugar in 4 quarts Boiling Water
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the Primary Sourdough Batter B according to recipe. Save 1 cup of starter separately. Let all ingredients come to room temperature.
2. Sift together 3/4 cup flour, salt, and sugar into a warm bowl.
3. Stir in salad oil and eggs.
4. Mix in the Primary Batter B and add enough additional flour for the dough to release easily from the sides of the bowl.
5. Turn the dough onto a well-floured surface and knead in enough additional flour to make it smooth and elastic (about 1/4 cup).
6. Place the dough in a warm, greased bowl, cover, and let rise in a warm place at 85°F until doubled in bulk (about 2 hours).
7. When doubled, punch down the dough. Let proof for an additional 1-1/2 hours or until doubled in bulk.
8. Turn the dough out onto a floured surface and divide it into 12-14 equal pieces.
9. Roll each piece into a 6-inch roll about 3/4 inch thick.
10. Pinch the two ends together to form a doughnut shape.
11. Bring 4 quarts of water to a boil and add 2 tablespoons sugar.
12. Drop each bagel into the boiling water one at a time. Boil in batches of no more than 4. Cook until they rise to the surface, then turn over and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
13. Remove with a slotted spoon and place on a greased cookie sheet.
14. When all have been boiled, transfer to a preheated oven set to 375°F and bake for 20-25 minutes until crusty and golden brown. Makes 12-14 bagels.
